title.zh: Why 500–600gsm Hoodies Are the New Streetwear Standard?
title.en: Why 500–600gsm Hoodies Are the New Streetwear Standard?

title.zh: The Shift Toward Premium Streetwear Identity
title.en: The Shift Toward Premium Streetwear Identity

description.zh: Streetwear is no longer just casual clothing—it has become a form of brand identity and cultural expression. In this shift, heavier hoodies (500–600gsm) are increasingly seen as a symbol of premium positioning. Brands use fabric weight as a visible and tactile signal of quality, allowing them to differentiate from mass-market fast fashion. A heavier hoodie immediately communicates “luxury streetwear” rather than basic apparel.
description.en: Streetwear is no longer just casual clothing—it has become a form of brand identity and cultural expression. In this shift, heavier hoodies (500–600gsm) are increasingly seen as a symbol of premium positioning. Brands use fabric weight as a visible and tactile signal of quality, allowing them to differentiate from mass-market fast fashion. A heavier hoodie immediately communicates “luxury streetwear” rather than basic apparel.

description.zh: One of the main reasons 500–600gsm hoodies are becoming standard is their ability to hold shape. Lighter hoodies often collapse or lose structure after washing, while heavyweight fabrics maintain a boxy, oversized silhouette that is highly desirable in modern streetwear. This structured fit enhances drop-shoulder designs and creates a more architectural look, which aligns with the aesthetic preferences of brands in the US, UK, and Australian markets.
description.en: One of the main reasons 500–600gsm hoodies are becoming standard is their ability to hold shape. Lighter hoodies often collapse or lose structure after washing, while heavyweight fabrics maintain a boxy, oversized silhouette that is highly desirable in modern streetwear. This structured fit enhances drop-shoulder designs and creates a more architectural look, which aligns with the aesthetic preferences of brands in the US, UK, and Australian markets.

description.zh: Heavyweight hoodies offer significantly improved durability. The denser fabric resists pilling, stretching, and shrinkage, making the garment last longer even under frequent wear. For consumers, this translates into better value per wear; for brands, it reduces quality complaints and strengthens customer satisfaction. In a market where sustainability and longevity are increasingly important, 500–600gsm fabrics align well with both performance and ethical consumption trends.
description.en: Heavyweight hoodies offer significantly improved durability. The denser fabric resists pilling, stretching, and shrinkage, making the garment last longer even under frequent wear. For consumers, this translates into better value per wear; for brands, it reduces quality complaints and strengthens customer satisfaction. In a market where sustainability and longevity are increasingly important, 500–600gsm fabrics align well with both performance and ethical consumption trends.

title.zh: Brand Positioning and Higher Retail Margins
title.en: Brand Positioning and Higher Retail Margins

description.zh: From a business perspective, heavier hoodies allow brands to justify higher retail prices. A 500–600gsm hoodie is often positioned as a “premium drop” product, enabling stronger profit margins compared to standard-weight garments. This also supports limited-edition releases and streetwear “drop culture,” where perceived scarcity and quality drive demand. As a result, heavyweight hoodies have become a strategic product category for emerging and established streetwear brands aiming to scale profitably.

subtitle.zh: 我们的成长历程
subtitle.en: Our Growth Journey
What They Say About Us

content.en: I am a purchasing manager for a Chinese supply chain. I first came across Google by chance. At first, I wasn't too sure if Kellogg could produce high-quality sweatshirts, especially those with more than 600 grams of fabric, because our company's style requires printing, embroidery, and even washing. To my surprise, they managed to do it, and at the same price point, the goods they delivered to us were of much better quality than those from other suppliers. I'm very glad to have met them and I will continue to work with them.

I have been collaborating with them for two years. Although their company is still relatively young, they have excellent quality control. For instance, their ability to analyze the understanding of heavyweight sweatshirts is something I greatly admire. Once, one of our company's products used a blend of polyester and cotton. The size of the sample was a little too small. However, the pre-production samples, the shipping samples, and even the final production goods were all handled very well by them. I discussed with them how they managed to do this. They said the size was a bit small because the fabric's shrinkage rate was unstable. Without asking me to increase the price, they washed the fabric to control the shrinkage rate, thus solving a big problem for me. In the future, I will continue to collaborate with them!

I highly recommend this company, especially for designers as us. They are extremely good at matching the appropriate fabrics based on the clients' requirements. My company mainly purchases T-shirts,Sweatshirts, and our boss has very high standards for the fabrics. Every time, they will send me very satisfactory fabric options according to my requests. Therefore, the styles of our company have always sold very well, and they also get the sizing exactly right. In summary, the supplier that can make our company profitable is the best choice!

Fabric shrinkage testing //

title.zh: Why American Brands Choose Kellogg Fashion?
title.en: Why American Brands Choose Kellogg Fashion?

description.zh: American streetwear culture places a premium on the "hand-feel" and structural integrity of a garment. Kellogg Fashion specializes in high-GSM (Grams per Square Meter) fabrics, often ranging from 400 GSM to over 500 GSM. Unlike standard suppliers, Kellogg’s textiles are engineered to be "stiff" and "dense" rather than just thick, providing the rugged, high-end aesthetic that US consumers associate with luxury streetwear brands.
description.en: American streetwear culture places a premium on the "hand-feel" and structural integrity of a garment. Kellogg Fashion specializes in high-GSM (Grams per Square Meter) fabrics, often ranging from 400 GSM to over 500 GSM. Unlike standard suppliers, Kellogg’s textiles are engineered to be "stiff" and "dense" rather than just thick, providing the rugged, high-end aesthetic that US consumers associate with luxury streetwear brands.

description.zh: The US market has moved away from slim or standard European cuts toward a very specific "Boxy Fit." This includes dropped shoulders, cropped body lengths, and wide sleeves. Kellogg Fashion has developed a deep library of pre-vetted patterns that specifically cater to this aesthetic. By providing "brand-ready" silhouettes, they allow American labels to skip the long, expensive process of multiple sampling rounds to get the fit right.

By providing "brand-ready" silhouettes, they allow American labels to skip the long, expensive process of multiple sampling rounds to get the fit right.

description.zh: The modern American fashion business model relies heavily on the "Drop" system—releasing limited quantities frequently to maintain hype. Kellogg Fashion offers a flexible, low-MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) framework that is highly attractive to both boutique labels and scaling brands. This agility allows American companies to test new colors and designs with minimal inventory risk, moving from a creative concept to a physical sample in as little as 7 to 10 days.

Oversized t-shirt fabrics are designed with higher GSM cotton construction to create a structured boxy silhouette, improved drape, and long-lasting shape retention. Unlike standard lightweight jersey fabrics, these materials prioritize density, stability, and premium streetwear aesthetics.

Common characteristics include:
- Heavyweight cotton (280gsm–380gsm)
- Compact yarn construction for stability
- Slightly dry or structured hand feel
- Minimal fabric twist after washing

title.zh: Analysis of Fabric Shrinkage Rate and Stability
title.en: Analysis of Fabric Shrinkage Rate and Stability

description.zh:
Shrinkage control is a critical factor in oversized t-shirt production. Standard untreated cotton fabrics may shrink between 3%–8% after washing, which affects fit consistency.

For premium streetwear production, stabilized cotton fabrics are used with:
- Pre-shrunk treatment
- Enzyme washing process
- Compact spinning yarn technology

Final shrinkage can be controlled within 1.5%–3%, ensuring consistent oversized fit across production batches.

question.zh: Q: What fabric is best for oversized t-shirts?
question.en: Q: What fabric is best for oversized t-shirts?

answer.zh: A: Heavyweight cotton fabrics between 280gsm and 380gsm are commonly used for oversized t-shirts because they provide better structure, improved drape, and long-lasting shape retention compared to lightweight jersey fabrics.
answer.en: A: Heavyweight cotton fabrics between 280gsm and 380gsm are commonly used for oversized t-shirts because they provide better structure, improved drape, and long-lasting shape retention compared to lightweight jersey fabrics.

question.zh: Q: Why do oversized t-shirts use heavyweight cotton?
question.en: Q: Why do oversized t-shirts use heavyweight cotton?

answer.zh: A: Heavyweight cotton helps oversized t-shirts maintain a boxy silhouette and premium streetwear appearance. Lighter fabrics often collapse or lose structure after washing, while heavyweight fabrics create a cleaner and more balanced fit.
answer.en: A: Heavyweight cotton helps oversized t-shirts maintain a boxy silhouette and premium streetwear appearance. Lighter fabrics often collapse or lose structure after washing, while heavyweight fabrics create a cleaner and more balanced fit.

question.en: Q: What GSM is recommended for streetwear oversized tees?

answer.zh: A: Most premium streetwear brands use 300gsm–350gsm cotton for oversized t-shirts. This weight range offers a balance between structure, comfort, durability, and oversized drape.
answer.en: A: Most premium streetwear brands use 300gsm–350gsm cotton for oversized t-shirts. This weight range offers a balance between structure, comfort, durability, and oversized drape.

question.zh: Q: Do heavyweight oversized t-shirts shrink after washing?
question.en: Q: Do heavyweight oversized t-shirts shrink after washing?

answer.zh: A: Shrinkage depends on fabric treatment and yarn construction. Pre-shrunk heavyweight cotton fabrics typically maintain shrinkage within 1.5%–3% after washing, helping preserve oversized fit consistency.
answer.en: A: Shrinkage depends on fabric treatment and yarn construction. Pre-shrunk heavyweight cotton fabrics typically maintain shrinkage within 1.5%–3% after washing, helping preserve oversized fit consistency.

question.zh: Q: What is the difference between regular cotton and compact cotton?
question.en: Q: What is the difference between regular cotton and compact cotton?

answer.zh: A: Compact cotton uses tighter yarn spinning technology to reduce hairiness, improve fabric density, and enhance shape retention. It is commonly used in premium oversized streetwear garments for a cleaner and more stable finish.
answer.en: A: Compact cotton uses tighter yarn spinning technology to reduce hairiness, improve fabric density, and enhance shape retention. It is commonly used in premium oversized streetwear garments for a cleaner and more stable finish.

question.zh: Q: Why do some oversized t-shirts lose shape easily?
question.en: Q: Why do some oversized t-shirts lose shape easily?

answer.zh: A: Oversized t-shirts may lose shape when low-density cotton fabrics or unstable knitting structures are used. Heavyweight compact cotton fabrics help improve garment stability and reduce deformation after repeated washing.
answer.en: A: Oversized t-shirts may lose shape when low-density cotton fabrics or unstable knitting structures are used. Heavyweight compact cotton fabrics help improve garment stability and reduce deformation after repeated washing.

answer.en: A: Yes. Garment dye fabrics are highly popular in oversized streetwear because they create vintage fade effects, softer textures, and more unique color depth compared to standard reactive dye fabrics.

title.zh: How We Make Your Tech Pack
title.en: How We Make Your Tech Pack

description.zh:
Every successful garment starts with a clear understanding of the brand’s vision. At Kellogg Fashion
, we carefully review your sketches, reference images, measurements, fabric requirements, and construction details before creating the tech pack. Our team communicates closely with clients to confirm oversized fit proportions, garment weight, sewing details, printing placement, washing effects, and overall streetwear aesthetics. This process helps reduce misunderstandings and ensures your product is production-ready from the beginning.
description.en:
Every successful garment starts with a clear understanding of the brand’s vision. At Kellogg Fashion
, we carefully review your sketches, reference images, measurements, fabric requirements, and construction details before creating the tech pack. Our team communicates closely with clients to confirm oversized fit proportions, garment weight, sewing details, printing placement, washing effects, and overall streetwear aesthetics. This process helps reduce misunderstandings and ensures your product is production-ready from the beginning.

description.zh: Once the design direction is confirmed, our development team builds a detailed tech pack with precise garment specifications. This includes measurements, size grading, stitching methods, fabric composition, GSM details, trim information, label placement, print dimensions, and sewing construction. For heavyweight hoodies and oversized streetwear garments, we also include important production details such as rib width, shoulder drop measurements, hood construction, and shrinkage tolerance to ensure consistency during bulk production.
description.en: Once the design direction is confirmed, our development team builds a detailed tech pack with precise garment specifications. This includes measurements, size grading, stitching methods, fabric composition, GSM details, trim information, label placement, print dimensions, and sewing construction. For heavyweight hoodies and oversized streetwear garments, we also include important production details such as rib width, shoulder drop measurements, hood construction, and shrinkage tolerance to ensure consistency during bulk production.

description.zh: Before sampling begins, we confirm all material and construction details with the client. Our team helps recommend suitable fabrics such as French terry, brushed fleece, heavyweight cotton jersey, or garment-dyed materials based on the desired hand-feel and fit. We also review printing techniques, embroidery methods, washing processes, and sewing construction to ensure the tech pack matches real production capabilities. This stage is important for avoiding production delays and maintaining product quality.
description.en: Before sampling begins, we confirm all material and construction details with the client. Our team helps recommend suitable fabrics such as French terry, brushed fleece, heavyweight cotton jersey, or garment-dyed materials based on the desired hand-feel and fit. We also review printing techniques, embroidery methods, washing processes, and sewing construction to ensure the tech pack matches real production capabilities. This stage is important for avoiding production delays and maintaining product quality.

description.zh: After the tech pack is finalized, we move into sample development and production preparation. During sampling, our team checks fit accuracy, sewing quality, fabric performance, and wash results against the original tech pack specifications. Any revisions are updated directly into the tech pack to create a clear production standard for bulk manufacturing. This process allows Kellogg Fashion to deliver stable quality, consistent sizing, and efficient production for global streetwear brands.

question.zh: Q1: How does Kellogg control shrinkage in 500gsm hoodies?
question.en: Q1: How does Kellogg control shrinkage in 500gsm hoodies?

answer.zh: A: We use pre-washed French Terry fabric and rigorous garment washing tests to ensure oversized fit retention.
answer.en: A: We use pre-washed French Terry fabric and rigorous garment washing tests to ensure oversized fit retention.

question.zh: Q2: What printing techniques are suitable for heavyweight hoodies?
question.en: Q2: What printing techniques are suitable for heavyweight hoodies?

answer.zh: A: DTG, puff print, and screen printing are optimized based on fabric weight and colorfastness.
answer.en: A: DTG, puff print, and screen printing are optimized based on fabric weight and colorfastness.

question.zh: Q3: What is the difference between French Terry and Fleece fabrics?
question.en: Q3: What is the difference between French Terry and Fleece fabrics?

answer.zh: A: French Terry is lighter, soft on the inside loop, suitable for oversized hoodies; Fleece is warmer, thicker, and provides more volume.
answer.en: A: French Terry is lighter, soft on the inside loop, suitable for oversized hoodies; Fleece is warmer, thicker, and provides more volume.

question.zh: Q4: How long does the Tech Pack approval process take?
question.en: Q4: How long does the Tech Pack approval process take?

answer.zh: A: Typically 3–5 business days, including size specification, fabric selection, and design verification.
answer.en: A: Typically 3–5 business days, including size specification, fabric selection, and design verification.

question.zh: Q5: Can Kellogg handle small batch custom orders?
question.en: Q5: Can Kellogg handle small batch custom orders?

answer.zh: A: Yes, we support small batches starting from 50–100 pieces per style per color.
answer.en: A: Yes, we support small batches starting from 50–100 pieces per style per color.
